rulings.Case Title: MORELIANO SANTOS CAVICII DZUL et al VS. BHATTI SATHAM SINGH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 9, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFFS MORELIANO DZUL, JUAN RIVAS, WILLIAM CAVICH, MARGARITO YEN, RONY DE LEON'S Motion To Enforce Settlement. DENIED. THE SUMMARY PROCEDURE TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENTS PERMITTED UNDER CCP SECTION 664.6 REQUIRES THAT THE AGREEMENT HAVE BEEN SIGNED BY THE PARTIES OR AGREED TO ORALLY IN COURT. THIS STATUTORY REQUIREMENT IS NOT MET HERE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MILLER et al VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Adn Motion By Pltf'S Richard Miller And Tamara Milller To Compel Deft Pacific Gas And Electric'S Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 9, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFFS RICHARD MILLER AND TAMARA MILLER Motion To Compel Defendant Pacific Gas And Electric's Production Of Documents. GRANTED AS TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1, REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS 5-8 AND 11-14, AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION 9, 19, 21, 25, 26 AND 29. DENIED AS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION 4 AND 30. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With A Court Order By Plaintiffs Richard Miller And Tamara Miller, And For Monetary, Issue And Evidence Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 9, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANTS PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY AND KEVIN SHAW'S Motion To Compel Compliance With A Court Order By Plaintiffs Richard Miller And Tamara Miller, And For Monetary, Issue And Evidence Sanctions. DENIED. ALL OF PLAINTIFFS' BELONGINGS WERE TO BE RETURNED BY NOVEMBER 5, 2010. PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT REQUIRED TO PRODUCE ITEMS NOT IN THEIR POSSESSION AND CONTROL. REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDY KILDAY EVENT PRODUCTION SERVICES, INC VS. SAN FRANCISCO MARITIME NATIONAL PARK ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Strike First Amended Complt; Req For Judicial Ntc; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 9, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO MARITIME NATIONAL PARK ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Strike First Amended Complaint. GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDY KILDAY EVENT PRODUCTION SERVICES, INC VS. SAN FRANCISCO MARITIME NATIONAL PARK ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 9, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT TWG, LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 1ST, 3RD, AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUPERFLUOUS IN LIGHT OF THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (CCP 1061). THE BREACH OF WRITTEN CONTRACT ACTION FAILS BECAUSE THE CONTRACT IS NOT PLED VERBATIM OR PLED ACCORDING TO ITS LEGAL INTENDMENT AND EFFECT. THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION IS TOTALLY CONCLUSORY AND DOES NOT COMPLY WITH THE PARTICULARITY REQUIREMENT. NUMEROUS OPPORTUNITIES TO AMEND HAVE BEEN PROVIDED AND NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. OVERRULED AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, COMMON COUNT, THAT MAY BE CHARACTERIZED AS QUANTUM MERUIT, QUASI CONTRACT, IMPLIED IN LAW CONTRACT, RESTITUTION, OR UNJUST ENRICHMENT. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THE PERFORMANCE OF SERVICES OR WORK AND LABOR AT DEFENDANTS' REQUEST WITH A PROMISE TO PAY, AND FAILURE TO PAY. SEE WITKIN, PLEADING, COMMON COUNT SECTION 566. 10 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. =(302/CWW)
